issue.author: mikaelholmen

comments.author: fhahn
comments.body:
I think the problem is GVN not properly invalidating the cache after replacing integer equalities (turning %_tmp47 = getelementptr [4 x i16], [4 x i16]* %ub.16, i16 0, i16 %step1.7.0 into %_tmp47 = getelementptr [4 x i16], [4 x i16]* %ub.16, i16 0, i16 0)

We should invalidate cached pointers also if any of their operands change: https://reviews.llvm.org/D65204

comments.author: fhahn
comments.body:
[Slightly reduced version of the reproducer](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/450489/143754968-0ecd3bab-4ef8-4dc5-a0df-8d1b735e374b.gz)
I had a look at this bug and it seems the problem is in MemoryDependenceAnalysis. For %_tmp79 = load i16, i16* %_tmp78 (  %_tmp78 = getelementptr [4 x i16], [4 x i16]* %ub.16, i16 0, i16 %i.8.0) we get the following defs:


%_tmp48 = load i16, i16* %_tmp47 (  %_tmp47 = getelementptr [4 x i16], [4 x i16]* %ub.16, i16 0, i16 %step1.7.0)
 
%_tmp58 = load i16, i16* %_tmp57 (  %_tmp57 = getelementptr [4 x i16], [4 x i16]* %ub.16, i16 0, i16 %step1.7.0


I think the reason that happens is related to caching. When looking at %_tmp48 and %_tmp58 respectively, we traverse their predecessors and do PHI translation along the way. When we reach the entry block, we translated their addresses to `getelementptr [4 x i16], [4 x i16]* %ub.16, i16 0, i16 0` and cache (Entry, getelementptr [4 x i16], [4 x i16]* %ub.16, i16 0, i16 0, %_tmp48/%_tmp57).

We do the same for %_tmp79 and also translate to `getelementptr [4 x i16], [4 x i16]* %ub.16, i16 0, i16 0`. Now we check the cache for exiting entries and return %_tmp48, %_tmp57.

I think caching the translated addresses is what is going wrong here. The translated addresses are only valid in the predecessor block they were translated for and it just means both loads access the same location along some path in the CFG (I think they would clobber each other, but not be a def). If we would place a load with the translated address in that block, it would be a def.

comments.author: preames
comments.body:
I'm suspecting this isn't a "simple" PRE bug.

I noticed by accident that running
"../build/bin/opt -gvn ../pre_gvn.ll -S -debug-only=gvn -enable-load-pre=0 | ../build/bin/opt -gvn -S" gives me different IR output than running "../build/bin/opt -gvn ../pre_gvn.ll -S -debug-only=gvn".  I would not expect that to be the case most of the time.  

Note that this is just a preliminary theory.  I need to spend more time analysing this before saying anything conclusive.

comments.author: mikaelholmen
comments.body:
> Mikael: is this a regression, or was it broken with llvm 3.9 as well as
> trunk?

Hi Hans,

It doesn't look like a recent regression.

As far as I can see it started going wrong on trunk already in September 2015 after commit:

    [PM/AA] Rebuild LLVM's alias analysis infrastructure in a way compatible
    with the new pass manager, and no longer relying on analysis groups.
    
    This builds essentially a ground-up new AA infrastructure stack for
    LLVM. The core ideas are the same that are used throughout the new pass
    manager: type erased polymorphism and direct composition.
    [...]
    Differential Revision: http://reviews.llvm.org/D12080
    
    git-svn-id: https://llvm.org/svn/llvm-project/llvm/trunk@247167 91177308-0d34-0410-b5e6-96231b3b80d8

comments.author: zmodem
comments.body: Mikael: is this a regression, or was it broken with llvm 3.9 as well as trunk?

comments.author: bjope
comments.body:
The reproducer for this ticket (as well as the modified version in the proposed patch that never landed, https://reviews.llvm.org/D65204) does not seem to work if enabling opaque-pointers.

No idea if that means that the actually bug goes away by enabling opaque pointers, or if it merely is that since `%_tmp47 = getelementptr [4 x i16], [4 x i16]* %ub.16, i16 0, i16 0)` would be rather pointless as one could just replace `%_tmp47` by `ptr %ub`. So maybe it depends on if GVN will simplify that first or not?

Btw, downstream we've been bailing out early from GVNPass::PerformLoadPRE for several years due to this bug. So we haven't included that function in any kind of test (neither normal regression or fuzzy testing) for very long time. If opaque pointers would imply that this bug could be solved, perhaps we could start enabling this in some testing to see that we do not find more faults in this area.

comments.author: fhahn
comments.body:
I think the bug is independent of opaque pointers. I pushed a test case that uses opaque pointers and performs incorrect PRE (it's possibly to verify that using alive2, it just requires ` -tv-src-unroll=2 -tv-tgt-unroll=2`): https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/411b9b8153ee95a51a2b7b3ebdb6fec9a7229f5a/llvm/test/Transforms/GVN/condprop-memdep-invalidation.ll

> Btw, downstream we've been bailing out early from GVNPass::PerformLoadPRE for several years due to this bug. So we haven't included that function in any kind of test (neither normal regression or fuzzy testing) for very long time. If opaque pointers would imply that this bug could be solved, perhaps we could start enabling this in some testing to see that we do not find more faults in this area.

That's unfortunate. I forgot about the patch, let's see if we can move forward with it. Note that there's currently work underway to transition GVN to use MemorySSA instead of the bug-prone MemDepAnalysis.

comments.author: mikaelholmen
comments.body:
Note: the testcase
```llvm/test/Transforms/GVN/condprop-memdep-invalidation.ll```
does not miscompile anymore after 4f046bc8e0b9
```[PHITranslateAddr] Require dominance when searching for translated address (PR57025)```

comments.author: mikaelholmen

issue.title: Wrong code bug after GVN/PRE

patch:
commit 4f046bc8e0b9439efeb2906ced7930f831495ee4
Author: Nikita Popov <npopov@redhat.com>
Date:   Tue Aug 30 14:26:36 2022 +0200

    [PHITranslateAddr] Require dominance when searching for translated address (PR57025)
    
    This is a fix for PR57025 and an alternative to D131776. The problem
    in the phi-translation-to-wrong-context.ll test case is that phi
    translation of %gep.j into if2 pick %gep.i as the result. While this
    instruction has the correct pointer address, it occurs in a context
    where %i != 0. As such, we get a NoAlias result for the store in
    if2, even though they do alias for %i == 0 (which is legal in the
    original context of the pointer).
    
    PHITranslateValue already has a MustDominate option, which can be
    used to restrict PHI translation results to values that dominate the
    translated-into block. However, this is more aggressive than what we
    need and would significantly regress GVN results. In particular, if
    we have a pointer value that does not require any translation, then
    it is fine to continue using that value in the predecessor, because
    the context is still correct for the original query. We only run into
    problems if PHITranslateSubExpr() picks a completely random
    instruction in a context that may have preconditions that do not hold.
    
    Fix this by always performing the dominance checks in
    PHITranslateSubExpr(), without enabling the more general MustDominate
    requirement.
    
    Fixes https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/57025. This also
    fixes the test case for https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/30999,
    but I'm not sure whether that's just the particular test case,
    or a general solution to the problem.
    
    Differential Revision: https://reviews.llvm.org/D132935

diff --git a/llvm/lib/Analysis/PHITransAddr.cpp b/llvm/lib/Analysis/PHITransAddr.cpp
index 9bd39345a9f5..4afacabd9e80 100644
--- a/llvm/lib/Analysis/PHITransAddr.cpp
+++ b/llvm/lib/Analysis/PHITransAddr.cpp
@@ -317,8 +317,7 @@ bool PHITransAddr::PHITranslateValue(BasicBlock *CurBB, BasicBlock *PredBB,
   assert(DT || !MustDominate);
   assert(Verify() && "Invalid PHITransAddr!");
   if (DT && DT->isReachableFromEntry(PredBB))
-    Addr =
-        PHITranslateSubExpr(Addr, CurBB, PredBB, MustDominate ? DT : nullptr);
+    Addr = PHITranslateSubExpr(Addr, CurBB, PredBB, DT);
   else
     Addr = nullptr;
   assert(Verify() && "Invalid PHITransAddr!");

tests.test_body:
target datalayout = "p:16:16"

; Function Attrs: memory(read)
declare void @use(i16) #0

define i16 @test_PR31651(ptr %ub.16) {
  br label %loop.1.header

loop.1.header:                                    ; preds = %loop.1.latch, %0
  %iv.1 = phi i16 [ 0, %0 ], [ %iv.1.next, %loop.1.latch ]
  %cmp.1 = icmp eq i16 %iv.1, 0
  br i1 %cmp.1, label %cont.1, label %then.1

then.1:                                           ; preds = %loop.1.header
  %_tmp18 = add i16 %iv.1, -1
  %gep.1 = getelementptr [4 x i16], ptr %ub.16, i16 1, i16 %_tmp18
  %l.1 = load i16, ptr %gep.1, align 2
  br label %cont.1

cont.1:                                           ; preds = %then.1, %loop.1.header
  %iv.1.sink = phi i16 [ %iv.1, %then.1 ], [ 0, %loop.1.header ]
  %sink = phi i16 [ %l.1, %then.1 ], [ 10, %loop.1.header ]
  %gep.2 = getelementptr [4 x i16], ptr %ub.16, i16 1, i16 %iv.1.sink
  %cmp.2 = icmp eq i16 %iv.1, 0
  br i1 %cmp.2, label %then.2, label %else.2

then.2:                                           ; preds = %cont.1
  %gep.3 = getelementptr [4 x i16], ptr %ub.16, i16 1, i16 %iv.1
  %l.2 = load i16, ptr %gep.3, align 2
  call void @use(i16 %l.2)
  br label %loop.1.latch

else.2:                                           ; preds = %cont.1
  %gep.4 = getelementptr [4 x i16], ptr %ub.16, i16 1, i16 %iv.1
  %l.3 = load i16, ptr %gep.4, align 2
  call void @use(i16 %l.3)
  br label %loop.1.latch

loop.1.latch:                                     ; preds = %else.2, %then.2
  %iv.1.next = add i16 %iv.1, 1
  %cmp.3 = icmp slt i16 %iv.1.next, 2
  br i1 %cmp.3, label %loop.1.header, label %loop.2

loop.2:                                           ; preds = %loop.2, %loop.1.latch
  %iv.2 = phi i16 [ 0, %loop.1.latch ], [ %iv.2.next, %loop.2 ]
  %sum = phi i16 [ 0, %loop.1.latch ], [ %sum.next, %loop.2 ]
  %gep.5 = getelementptr [4 x i16], ptr %ub.16, i16 1, i16 %iv.2
  %l.4 = load i16, ptr %gep.5, align 2
  %sum.next = add i16 %sum, %l.4
  %iv.2.next = add i16 %iv.2, 1
  %cmp.4 = icmp slt i16 %iv.2.next, 2
  br i1 %cmp.4, label %loop.2, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.2
  ret i16 %sum.next
}

attributes #0 = { memory(read) }

declare void @verify(i16)

define void @test(i16 %g) {
entry:
  %arr = alloca [4 x i16], align 1
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %epilog, %entry
  %i = phi i16 [ 0, %entry ], [ %next.i, %epilog ]
  %cmp0 = icmp eq i16 %i, 0
  br i1 %cmp0, label %store.idx.0, label %store.idx.i

store.idx.0:                                      ; preds = %for.body
  store i16 7, ptr %arr, align 1
  br label %store.done

store.idx.i:                                      ; preds = %for.body
  %arr.i = getelementptr [4 x i16], ptr %arr, i16 0, i16 %i
  store i16 42, ptr %arr.i, align 1
  br label %store.done

store.done:                                       ; preds = %store.idx.i, %store.idx.0
  br label %while.body

while.body:                                       ; preds = %while.body, %store.done
  %j = phi i16 [ %i, %store.done ], [ 0, %while.body ]
  br i1 false, label %while.body, label %while.end

while.end:                                        ; preds = %while.body
  %arr.j = getelementptr [4 x i16], ptr %arr, i16 0, i16 %j
  %value = load i16, ptr %arr.j, align 1
  tail call void @verify(i16 %value)
  br label %epilog

epilog:                                           ; preds = %while.end
  %next.i = add i16 %j, 1
  %arr.next.i = getelementptr [4 x i16], ptr %arr, i16 0, i16 %next.i
  %dead = load i16, ptr %arr.next.i, align 1
  %cmp4 = icmp slt i16 %next.i, 3
  br i1 %cmp4, label %for.body, label %for.end

for.end:                                          ; preds = %epilog
  ret void
}

define i32 @test(i64 %i, i1 %c1, i1 %c2) {
entry:
  %ptr = alloca [2 x i32], align 4
  br i1 %c1, label %if, label %else

if:                                               ; preds = %entry
  br i1 %c2, label %if2, label %else2

if2:                                              ; preds = %if
  store i32 1, ptr %ptr, align 4
  br label %join

else2:                                            ; preds = %if
  store i32 2, ptr %ptr, align 4
  br label %join

join:                                             ; preds = %else2, %if2
  %j = phi i64 [ %i, %if2 ], [ 0, %else2 ]
  %gep.j = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %ptr, i64 %j
  %v = load i32, ptr %gep.j, align 4
  ret i32 %v

else:                                             ; preds = %entry
  %cmp = icmp ne i64 %i, 0
  call void @llvm.assume(i1 %cmp)
  %gep.i = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %ptr, i64 %i
  ret i32 0
}

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(inaccessiblemem: write)
declare void @llvm.assume(i1 noundef) #0

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(inaccessiblemem: write) }
